segments.text: Then answered Zophar the Naamathite, and said,
Should not the multitude of words be answered? and should a man full of talk be justified?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Should not the multitude of words be answered? and should a man full of talk be justified?
segments.speaker: Zophar

footnotes: full of talk: Heb. of lips

segments.text: Should thy lies make men hold their peace? and when thou mockest, shall no man make thee ashamed?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: My doctrine is pure, and I am clean in thine eyes.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Oh that God would speak, and open his lips against thee;
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: And that he would shew thee the secrets of wisdom, that they are double to that which is! Know therefore that God exacteth of thee less than thine iniquity deserveth.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Canst thou by searching find out God? canst thou find out the Almighty unto perfection?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: It is as high as heaven; what canst thou do? deeper than hell; what canst thou know?
segments.speaker: Zophar

footnotes: as high as heaven: Heb. the heights of heaven

segments.text: The measure thereof is longer than the earth, and broader than the sea.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: If he cut off, and shut up, or gather together, then who can hinder him?
segments.speaker: Zophar

footnotes: cut off: or, make a change
hinder him: Heb. turn him away?

segments.text: For he knoweth vain men: he seeth wickedness also; will he not then consider it?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: For vain man would be wise, though man be born like a wild ass's colt.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: If thou prepare thine heart, and stretch out thine hands toward him;
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: If iniquity be in thine hand, put it far away, and let not wickedness dwell in thy tabernacles.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: For then shalt thou lift up thy face without spot; yea, thou shalt be stedfast, and shalt not fear:
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Because thou shalt forget thy misery, and remember it as waters that pass away:
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: And thine age shall be clearer than the noonday; thou shalt shine forth, thou shalt be as the morning.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: And thou shalt be secure, because there is hope; yea, thou shalt dig about thee, and thou shalt take thy rest in safety.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Also thou shalt lie down, and none shall make thee afraid; yea, many shall make suit unto thee.
segments.speaker: Zophar

footnotes: make suit: Heb. intreat thy face

segments.text: But the eyes of the wicked shall fail, and they shall not escape, and their hope shall be as the giving up of the ghost.
segments.speaker: Zophar

footnotes: they shall not escape: Heb. flight shall perish from them
the giving up of the ghost: or, a puff of breath
